DMSTAr 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Changelog

LPWEM internal helper functions

Source: R/lpwem.R
lpwem-internal.Rd

Internal building blocks used by the LPWEM (Lake Phosphorus Wetland Ecosystem Model) implementation for transport and kinetic updates across a tanks-in-series wetland representation.

Converts an areal flux expressed in \(mg m^{-2} d^{-1}\) to a mass rate in \(kg d^{-1}\) by multiplying by area in \(km^2\).

Constructs an equal-area tanks-in-series (TIS) representation for a wetland cell, where each tank has area \(A_i = A_{cell}/N\).

Computes the LPWEM return flux \(r^*_i\) (\(mg m^{-2} d^{-1}\)) across a tanks-in-series chain, linearly interpolated from the first tank value r1_mgm2d to the last tank value rN_mgm2d.

Computes the LPWEM areal uptake flux \(J_i\) (\(mg m^{-2} d^{-1}\)) from tank concentrations and hydrologic forcing using: $$J = k_0 \left(1 + \alpha \frac{Q}{W}\right) TP^2$$ where \(Q\) is in \(m^3 d^{-1}\), \(W\) is in \(m\), and \(TP\) is in \(ppb\) (treated as \(mg m^{-3}\)). Unit consistency is carried by \(k_0\).

Constructs the sequence \(Q_i\) for \(i = 0..N\) (length \(N+1\)) by linear interpolation between wetland inflow \(Q_0\) and wetland outflow \(Q_N\).

Advances the tank TP concentration state one micro time step using an explicit Euler update of the mass-balance formulation across a tanks-in-series chain.

lpwem_areal_to_kgd(rate_mgm2d, A_km2)

lpwem_build_tis(A_cell_km2, N = 6L)

lpwem_rstar_vec(N, r1_mgm2d, rN_mgm2d)

lpwem_J_vec(TP_ppb, Q_hm3d, W_m, k0, alpha)

lpwem_Q_along_tanks(Q0_hm3d, QN_hm3d, N)

lpwem_tp_euler_step(TP, Vavg_hm3, Q0_hm3d, QN_hm3d, tis, pars, dt_day)

Arguments

rate_mgm2d

Numeric areal rate in \(mg m^{-2} d^{-1}\).

A_km2

Numeric area in \(km^2\).

A_cell_km2

Total wetland surface area in \(km^2\).

N

Integer number of tanks.

r1_mgm2d

Return flux at the first tank (\(mg m^{-2} d^{-1}\)).

rN_mgm2d

Return flux at the last tank (\(mg m^{-2} d^{-1}\)).

TP_ppb

Numeric vector of tank TP concentrations (ppb).

Q_hm3d

Numeric vector of tank flows (\(hm^3 d^{-1}\)), typically the outflow from each tank.

W_m

Numeric wetland width parameter (\(m\)).

k0

Numeric uptake coefficient \(k_0\).

alpha

Numeric coefficient \(\alpha\) applied to \(Q/W\).

Q0_hm3d

Wetland inflow rate (\(hm^3 d^{-1}\)).

QN_hm3d

Wetland outflow rate (\(hm^3 d^{-1}\)).

TP

Numeric vector of length tis$N of tank TP concentrations (ppb).

Vavg_hm3

Step-average wetland volume (\(hm^3\)).

tis

Tanks-in-series structure as returned by lpwem_build_tis().

pars

List of LPWEM parameters; must include Cin_ppb, W_m, k0, alpha, r1_mgm2d, rN_mgm2d.

dt_day

Micro-step duration in days.

Value

Numeric mass rate in \(kg d^{-1}\).

A list with:

  • N: integer number of tanks

  • A_i: numeric vector of length N of tank areas (\(km^2\))

Numeric vector of length N with the return flux for each tank.

Numeric vector of uptake flux \(J_i\) in \(mg m^{-2} d^{-1}\).

Numeric vector of length N + 1 giving Q_0, ..., Q_N

in \(hm^3 d^{-1}\).

A list with:

  • TP_new: updated TP vector (ppb)

  • diag: diagnostics including h_m, Q_along, J_mgm2d, r_mgm2d, and per-tank mass-balance terms

Details

These functions are not intended to be called directly by end users.

The conversion is implicit because: $$(mg/m^2/day) * (km^2) = (mg/m^2/day) * (10^6 m^2) = 10^6 mg/day = 1 kg/day.$$

The implementation updates per-tank mass \(M_i\) and converts back to concentration. Water depth is approximated as: $$h = V/A$$ with \(V\) in \(hm^3\) and \(A\) in \(km^2\), yielding \(h\) in meters because \(1\,m * 1\,km^2 = 1\,hm^3\).

Flow is interpolated along tanks from \(Q_0\) to \(Q_N\), and the return flux \(r^*\) is interpolated linearly from the first to last tank values.

Examples

if (FALSE) {
lpwem_build_tis(12, N = 6)
}
if (FALSE) {
lpwem_rstar_vec(N = 4, r1_mgm2d = 10, rN_mgm2d = 2)
}
if (FALSE) {
lpwem_Q_along_tanks(Q0_hm3d = 2, QN_hm3d = 1, N = 4)
}

Contents
